name: Mark Robinson
subject: Can I share a quick story?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Robinson
body:
I wasn't born with a silver spoon in my mouth.

| |
---
| | | | | I grew up as the 9th of 10 children in Greensboro. My father was an alcoholic who beat my mother. He died when I was in the fifth grade, and I was terrified. He had been the sole provider for our household. I remember sitting on the porch wondering where I'd be in five years.

Would I be in a shelter?

In foster care?

Would I be separated from my brothers and sisters?
---
| [](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=98ea98a06e474850a532392ab2a0de51&_e=-xjknwUl7NDLDDGRxVyBTUgLyM27Hx7XZQ1-YUeGPOIRLhL8FIw_lmtMVBreDvNH0u4y3NSIBaue0MrXDrQg7zPCbLTcPrLvbX5A0X2I5st3mJQD0yiTYyRO0mDQ6lg1a5QpflpyDlnbzKwT7zg8jBzbaILGidYeWi3DPQVC2WnpkHQrjNZDzrGWB7pWoi-T0SzyGBArGlDf_mGIdU1mU5cTrpSY8phn6zGdFyAsS64Z3XieQhxQqV2EH1u3AGh8pU9lj0bq5ucS8vzr3GAuFTwJlOwey3f5i7RVzEayOlyv5a4GIxEm1p1LrSUpiNGpdm6n-aXrerXuS1ldn6c7Cibz2voLf4ev__r_A-o1am7pYfq8DS2XE6JbnyPtXty0iCWXvDO6Ma3Bz333jK9kyg4YOqmbYMznkQSZv8NPD7-rFm6LTsemqJHllpwQJ4jsER9HnG6N1aLbsQfxJJP6Tg%3D%3D)
---

A few days after my father died, my mom's friend came by. She explained that now that her husband was gone, my mother could kick her feet up and relax because the government would take care of everything.

But that wasn't my mom.

As soon as her friend left, she got up and walked over to the local university and got a job as a janitor. She had a fifth-grade education and five kids to provide for. She chose to clean up for other kids to provide for her own. **She didn 't just rise to the challenge, she excelled. **

She gave a sense of calm and purpose to us. From that point on, we always had what we needed... _very little of what we wanted, but always what we needed. _

My mother was and always will be my hero. She showed me what strength and perseverance looked like first hand, and she taught me that with faith and hard work, I could achieve anything.

Those early years of hardship were just the beginning of my struggles.

My wife and I have endured a lot. I lost not one, but two jobs due to NAFTA.

We lost homes, cars, and were even forced into bankruptcy.

I remember lying awake at night wondering how I was going to pay the bills.

_**But I never forgot what my mother taught me, and we never gave up. **_

Together, my wife of over 30 years and I persevered and raised two incredible children. Now we have two amazing grandkids.

---
| [](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=98ea98a06e474850a532392ab2a0de51&_e=-xjknwUl7NDLDDGRxVyBTUgLyM27Hx7XZQ1-YUeGPOIRLhL8FIw_lmtMVBreDvNH0u4y3NSIBaue0MrXDrQg7zPCbLTcPrLvbX5A0X2I5st3mJQD0yiTYyRO0mDQ6lg1a5QpflpyDlnbzKwT7zg8jBzbaILGidYeWi3DPQVC2WnpkHQrjNZDzrGWB7pWoi-T0SzyGBArGlDf_mGIdU1mU5cTrpSY8phn6zGdFyAsS64Z3XieQhxQqV2EH1u3AGh8pU9lj0bq5ucS8vzr3GAuFTwJlOwey3f5i7RVzEayOlyv5a4GIxEm1p1LrSUpiNGpFZi9yNvTWp8u-cJDfEPN_c2Ey7YTLDdX1rQ3hq_deikiTRUKg1mavMlKZR4kQvmSAcft5K9uY2yNTuFY7L2C0djI3QXQdKVyLIbaeYZIHB7SiQAsw_lcqd9tBJDO8efLe1vcN4clbaZDrJaevhWPIA%3D%3D)
---

In 2018, my life changed when I gave a speech to the Greensboro city council that went viral.

A year later, I launched my campaign for Lieutenant Governor, and when I was elected in 2020, _**I became the first black Lieutenant Governor of North Carolina. **_

As Lieutenant Governor, I have had the privilege to work on a lot of issues, but my main focus has been on education.

It is a passion of mine, and the reason I ran for Lieutenant Governor. I have been a vocal proponent of parents knowing what their children are being taught. I have supported providing opportunities for all students to have an education that best suits them, whether that's in a private school, charter school, home school, or public school.

---
| [](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=98ea98a06e474850a532392ab2a0de51&_e=-xjknwUl7NDLDDGRxVyBTUgLyM27Hx7XZQ1-YUeGPOIRLhL8FIw_lmtMVBreDvNH0u4y3NSIBaue0MrXDrQg7zPCbLTcPrLvbX5A0X2I5st3mJQD0yiTYyRO0mDQ6lg1a5QpflpyDlnbzKwT7zg8jBzbaILGidYeWi3DPQVC2WnpkHQrjNZDzrGWB7pWoi-T0SzyGBArGlDf_mGIdU1mU5cTrpSY8phn6zGdFyAsS64Z3XieQhxQqV2EH1u3AGh8pU9lj0bq5ucS8vzr3GAuFTwJlOwey3f5i7RVzEayOlyv5a4GIxEm1p1LrSUpiNGpw7S52ZpTUDOF6mYM3tUHMIp4gf0m-J-6OXl-GqwvRkS9P2NCTupLVPMo5aacaox1tIgYWksfP6kArLoBBrwTbA5bA1db7R8jajul5KMIMn6jxTrpTpI-mWzIeY0zjuZRwahS6lG_WsnOyNvBntuwKQ%3D%3D)

Now, I've thrown my hat in the ring to be the next Governor of North Carolina. Not because I want to be Governor.

My campaign for Governor is not about want. _**IT 'S ABOUT SERVICE.**_

Some day, you and I will be gone. And I don't want my family to see a picture of me and wonder why I didn't fight for them. I want my children and grandchildren to know that I did everything I could. That's what I'm fighting for. And that's why I'm running for Governor.

Now, I'm asking for your help.

_**This is going to be the most contested and most expensive governor 's race in the country.**_ As North Carolina goes, the nation goes. So I hope you will consider making a contribution to my campaign today. _**[Whether it's $500 or just $5, every single donation will get us one step closer to making history.](https://links.freedomnationtoday.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=98ea98a06e474850a532392ab2a0de51&_e=-xjknwUl7NDLDDGRxVyBTUgLyM27Hx7XZQ1-YUeGPOIRLhL8FIw_lmtMVBreDvNH0u4y3NSIBaue0MrXDrQg7zPCbLTcPrLvbX5A0X2I5st3mJQD0yiTYyRO0mDQ6lg1a5QpflpyDlnbzKwT7zg8jBzbaILGidYeWi3DPQVC2WnpkHQrjNZDzrGWB7pWoi-T0SzyGBArGlDf_mGIdU1mU5cTrpSY8phn6zGdFyAsS64Z3XieQhxQqV2EH1u3AGh8pU9lj0bq5ucS8vzr3GAuFTwJlOwey3f5i7RVzEayOlyv5a4GIxEm1p1LrSUpiNGplnULoVit_jstWZB_N7r1_LbXdIob4IFTKsLMtzb4O9o9PE3aWAYpZcwegSE6oKwYcBnkIfT-hpCjmF8lpqoLCrWSlkS3WcdmCy-Jse-B0in7nLjb9xAMVxXUPMowGjrHLqtvJD6_WwSu_D0E3uJ5Ww%3D%3D "Whether it’s $500 or just $5, every single donation will get us one step closer to making history.")**_

clean_body: I wasn't born with a silver spoon in my mouth. | | | | | | | I grew up as the 9th of 10 children in Greensboro. My father was an alcoholic who beat my mother. He died when I was in the fifth grade, and I was terrified. He had been the sole provider for our household. I remember sitting on the porch wondering where I'd be in five years. Would I be in a shelter? In foster care? Would I be separated from my brothers and sisters? | A few days after my father died, my mom's friend came by. She explained that now that her husband was gone, my mother could kick her feet up and relax because the government would take care of everything. But that wasn't my mom. As soon as her friend left, she got up and walked over to the local university and got a job as a janitor. She had a fifth-grade education and five kids to provide for. She chose to clean up for other kids to provide for her own. She didn 't just rise to the challenge, she excelled. She gave a sense of calm and purpose to us. From that point on, we always had what we needed... very little of what we wanted, but always what we needed. My mother was and always will be my hero. She showed me what strength and perseverance looked like first hand, and she taught me that with faith and hard work, I could achieve anything. Those early years of hardship were just the beginning of my struggles. My wife and I have endured a lot. I lost not one, but two jobs due to NAFTA. We lost homes, cars, and were even forced into bankruptcy. I remember lying awake at night wondering how I was going to pay the bills. But I never forgot what my mother taught me, and we never gave up. Together, my wife of over 30 years and I persevered and raised two incredible children. Now we have two amazing grandkids. | In 2018, my life changed when I gave a speech to the Greensboro city council that went viral. A year later, I launched my campaign for Lieutenant Governor, and when I was elected in 2020, I became the first black Lieutenant Governor of North Carolina. As Lieutenant Governor, I have had the privilege to work on a lot of issues, but my main focus has been on education. It is a passion of mine, and the reason I ran for Lieutenant Governor. I have been a vocal proponent of parents knowing what their children are being taught. I have supported providing opportunities for all students to have an education that best suits them, whether that's in a private school, charter school, home school, or public school. | Now, I've thrown my hat in the ring to be the next Governor of North Carolina. Not because I want to be Governor. My campaign for Governor is not about want. IT 'S ABOUT SERVICE. Some day, you and I will be gone. And I don't want my family to see a picture of me and wonder why I didn't fight for them. I want my children and grandchildren to know that I did everything I could. That's what I'm fighting for. And that's why I'm running for Governor. Now, I'm asking for your help. This is going to be the most contested and most expensive governor 's race in the country. As North Carolina goes, the nation goes. So I hope you will consider making a contribution to my campaign today. "Whether it’s $500 or just $5, every single donation will get us one step closer to making history." | | | Thanks in advance for your support, donations, and prayers. | Mark Robinson | | Paid for by Friends of Mark Robinson Business donations are not allowed. name: Daniel Cooper
subject: oh crab!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alsobrooks for Senate
body:
[ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/4682?t=1009&amount=25&akid=326%2E52803%2EFcMg1U ]Daniel Cooper holding a crab

Hi all! It’s Daniel, Angela’s Deputy Campaign Manager.

I don’t mean to cause any Sunday Scaries, but I woke up feeling extra
crabby today.

You see, tonight marks the end of the first week of August. And I just
logged on to find out we’re currently on pace for one of our slowest
fundraising weeks of Angela’s entire campaign!

I’m usually pretty optimistic. But I’m also a realist—and right now, we’re
going to need a BIG fundraising day to make sure we don’t fall too far
behind on our August fundraising.

[ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/4682?t=1010&amount=25&akid=326%2E52803%2EFcMg1U ]So, could you help me out,
Peter? All I’m asking for is a $5,
$10 or even $25 donation to support our Senate campaign before you enjoy
the rest of your Sunday!

[ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/4682?t=1011&amount=25&akid=326%2E52803%2EFcMg1U ]Donate Now!

When you’re running a Senate campaign against a self-funded,
multi-millionaire who has invested nearly $10 million in his campaign, you
can’t miss a single fundraising benchmark, Peter.

That’s why I’m having to review our campaign’s weekly fundraising numbers
first thing—and why I’m reaching out to loyal supporters like you to ask
for help.

Our voter contact efforts, community events, and ability to run ads all
come down to whether or not we have the resources to spread our message
into every corner of Maryland.

[ https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/4682?t=1012&amount=25&akid=326%2E52803%2EFcMg1U ]So please,
Peter, will you chip in
whatever amount you can spare to help get our fundraising numbers back on
track and elect a leader who always puts Maryland first?

Thanks for your help,

Daniel Cooper

name: Andy Kim
subject: Good to be home
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andy Kim for Congress
body:
With the school year over, I decided to try something different and have my family down with me in DC for part of July as we had birthdays to celebrate and to give my boys some different experiences. My 6 and 7 year olds ran amok around the Capitol, saw probably every Smithsonian museum, and even got to play catch on a major league field!

We made a lot of great memories.

https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-andy-kim?refcode=2023-08-06-goinghome&recurring=&amount=3&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=9bdad5e1-5e34-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/stand-with-andy-kim?refcode=2023-08-06-goinghome&recurring=&amount=3&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=9bdad5e1-5e34-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73]

But it's great to be back in Jersey to enjoy the remainder of summer.

They're looking forward to seeing their neighborhood friends, riding bikes around the neighborhood, going to camp in the pinelands, and of course, rummaging through their giant bin of miscellaneous Lego pieces. We love the simple life.

I'm so glad my boys have New Jersey to call home, just as I did when I was their age.

They are why I do the work. So my kids and your kids and grandkids can grow up safe, happy, and healthy in America.

More soon,

Andy

name: Team Evergreen
subject: The one climate action you need to take this year
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY EVERGREEN COLLABORATIVE
body:
We can't miss this chance
| | | | | [](https://act.evergreenaction.com/go/3139?t=2&akid=13588%2E53494%2Ehr0u8j)
---
| | Friend, The excruciating heat most of us have endured the past month? It's not normal. **In fact, we're currently experiencing the hottest summer ever recorded—and it's a direct result of climate change. ** The power sector is the second largest contributor to climate pollution, and it's intensifying all of these heatwaves, floods, and wildfires. One of our best chances to ramp down this pollution is through EPA's proposed regulations on climate pollution from power plants. **Today is your last opportunity to tell EPA to finalize the strongest possible version of these rules**. It only takes a second.
**[We've already drafted the comment, so all you need to do is join the 55,000+ other advocates who've signed on and add your name.](https://act.evergreenaction.com/letter/EPA-clean-up-climate-pollution/?source=ema_2023_08_06_111rules_lastchance&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=100clean&utm_source=email2023_08_06&t=3&akid=13588%2E53494%2Ehr0u8j)**
---
[**SIGN ON NOW**](https://act.evergreenaction.com/letter/EPA-clean-up-climate-pollution/?source=ema_2023_08_06_111rules_lastchance&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=100clean&utm_source=email2023_08_06&t=5&akid=13588%2E53494%2Ehr0u8j)
---
Fossil fuel plants and Big Polluters are pushing back, which means these rules are going one of two ways: They're going to get stronger, or they're going to get weaker. **The difference could be us.**
**[It's going to take all of us, so if you take one action for our climate, let it be this one.](https://act.evergreenaction.com/letter/EPA-clean-up-climate-pollution/?source=ema_2023_08_06_111rules_lastchance&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=100clean&utm_source=email2023_08_06&t=6&akid=13588%2E53494%2Ehr0u8j)**
---
[**I'M IN—ADD MY SIGNATURE**](https://act.evergreenaction.com/letter/EPA-clean-up-climate-pollution/?source=ema_2023_08_06_111rules_lastchance&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=100clean&utm_source=email2023_08_06&t=8&akid=13588%2E53494%2Ehr0u8j)
---
In solidarity, Evergreen Collaborative
---

name: Team Kilmer
subject: Voting suppression of any kind is a threat to our democracy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by People for Derek Kilmer
body:
The Brennan Center for Justice found, as of June 2023, legislators in at least 11 states have enacted 13 restrictive bills that would undermine the electoral process.
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/67664178/420453886/-2027002553?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDMzNzUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNjMzYTA2ODktNjEzNC1lZTExLWEzZjEtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Tu8ul2EOZt1Hkd82wn7RrXnAvS1-yiJOGGu5AiRC3wc=&emci=5c6cde89-fc32-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&emdi=633a0689-6134-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&ceid=3595835)

Peter — Let's be clear: Voter suppression of _any kind_ is a threat to our democracy.

But unfortunately, lawmakers continue to introduce brazen and oppressive voting legislation focused on restricting and disproportionately sabotaging voters of color, seniors, and young people.

**The Brennan Center for Justice found, as of June 2023, legislators in at least 11 states have enacted 13 restrictive bills that would undermine the electoral process.**

Derek believes that Congress should protect the rights of every American citizen to participate in our democracy. And it should act before more voter suppression laws are implemented. That's why he is a proud co-sponsor of the Freedom to Vote Act – a bill that will strengthen the voice of the American people in their democracy by making it easier to vote, ending the dominance of big money in the political process, and ending partisan gerrymandering.

**[We can't let extreme politicians roll back voter protections and rights to further their own dangerous, self-serving agendas. Add your name to this petition to support the Freedom to Vote Act>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/67664179/420453887/700284519?ms=em23_230806_VRP_fnd_gq_act___TK_ettx&refcode=em23_230806_VRP_fnd_gq_act___TK_ettx&source=em23_230806_VRP_fnd_gq_act___TK_ettx&utm_campaign=em23_230806_VRP_fnd_gq_act___TK_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=kyFSrYrtr+5d4/Qe9mSHcqG1mEgA5Hw1cFZZY4Hg94y2Yg9njp9keEL+LVKDcLB1ENA5F1DI22OE/EyBC9ff0rOGm3GT80OLj0TqBPePkyDmBGc7QCphtVADuvJdu6Fmr5nWtbpkQoz05X4Z22B7sN76Pbt7NDOkckmi1iE6boI=&refcode2=633a0689-6134-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDMzNzUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNjMzYTA2ODktNjEzNC1lZTExLWEzZjEtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Tu8ul2EOZt1Hkd82wn7RrXnAvS1-yiJOGGu5AiRC3wc=&emci=5c6cde89-fc32-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&emdi=633a0689-6134-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&ceid=3595835)**

Trump; (iii) two (2) day one (1) night first class hotel accommodations (1 room, double occupancy); (iv) one (1) photograph of the Prize Winner and guest with President Trump; approximate retail value ("ARV") of the Prize: $5,000. The Promotion is open only to U.S. citizens or lawful permanent U.S. residents who are legal residents (green card holders) of the 50 United States, Puerto Rico, and the District of Columbia and are at least 18 years of age (or the age of majority under applicable law). Winners must be 18 or older (or of majority under applicable law) and meet other requirements as described in the Official Rules. Promotion subject to Official Rules and additional restrictions on eligibility may apply. Visit for full details and Official Rules. Sponsor: Trump Save America, PO Box 13570, Arlington, VA 22219. name: Reclaiming American Freedom
subject: Lincoln's secret weapon
body:
Asked by a reporter in 1860 how he had acquired his extraordinary rhetorical skills
%recipient.md5_hash%
| This sponsored message from Reclaiming American Freedom is on behalf of Hillsdale College | | [](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=a15c26d043074d7ba3a99f8ca17d57d0&_e=nScp6_ExYvgConJGLzHsLP1NQBMVx8WGOSrNQ0-tQ3UjLfFo-K4K-B_ufeRHXmfJjmstPnJ9q8zXF4-njMJx637XrYWSua2xMw862VRdqg1UPadU2xkhUpFJeasnzprgaP3kMXCPlSz_mWBcRIuWukYA0uXgY6jueEdYm9YGjQmemyn4YIzyJSLT2Qd79Aj0td53OPONb2Mj3WCQdxKJcrVnUdc7sA9Y3h4OXnU1I-bl865OesHKhHyON3tyt7Laa_sCuFK_owQuUthOTTGml1aVf1VIAKut0jqHmjYTaGjheRN_tOHIySfHmjL_rr5EPet0SFd9Ajf3p6jDXnzg1Sz8VgZjPnW0bzmrN1nGlzq5XKoeAFTtX-EWGXCVFNPnFjYB96jqrMdNC_KovzOD486Cb8r37nWDsOZrH2IcDIQ%3D)
---
| | Dear fellow American, Asked by a reporter in 1860 how he had acquired his extraordinary rhetorical skills, Abraham Lincoln credited his study of Euclid. While studying law in Springfield, Lincoln explained, he became convinced that the ability to demonstrate the truth of things was essential to the good practice of law. Consequently, Lincoln left his job, retired to his father's cabin, and did not return until he could "give any proposition in the six books of Euclid at sight." By studying Euclid, we improve our ability to reason soundly--plus, in my experience, the study of Euclid can be great fun. That's why Hillsdale College has produced a free online course on the topic **" Mathematics and Logic: From Euclid to Modern Geometry." ** Through** "Mathematics and Logic: From Euclid to Modern Geometry,"** you will learn the importance of deductive reasoning as a foundation for pursuing knowledge in all fields of learning, as well as in everyday life.
---
| | **Now, the course is available in a beautiful DVD box set for viewing in your home or for use in a small group.** ** ** **You can receive the DVD box set as a thank-you for your tax-deductible gift of $100 or more in support of our educational outreach efforts on behalf of liberty.** Your tax-deductible gift will help promote our free online courses and other educational outreach efforts in this critical time for our nation. And your gift matters even more because... Hillsdale accomplishes all its work while refusing to accept ONE PENNY of government funding--not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans.
---
| [](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=a15c26d043074d7ba3a99f8ca17d57d0&_e=nScp6_ExYvgConJGLzHsLP1NQBMVx8WGOSrNQ0-tQ3UjLfFo-K4K-B_ufeRHXmfJjmstPnJ9q8zXF4-njMJx637XrYWSua2xMw862VRdqg1UPadU2xkhUpFJeasnzprgaP3kMXCPlSz_mWBcRIuWukYA0uXgY6jueEdYm9YGjQmemyn4YIzyJSLT2Qd79Aj0td53OPONb2Mj3WCQdxKJcrVnUdc7sA9Y3h4OXnU1I-bl865OesHKhHyON3tyt7LaxdkWIDfmzTdV7R2n2IWzmME-rxvGFfNea-oo9ou73X_-kApqF7a1hFvsiob5SMDIPwFlPMHgV7cVIXUwhUKCkgv6xQF7KM0JteGWrN1Csrng1F9c20BV5VO_YaODRF6WVOvM3r3gw6_Nhw3bMLd-NLZdipjoJBmVnFAVaY3roRo%3D)
---
| | That's why we need the support of friends like you, who understand the importance of education to liberty. Quantities of this special DVD box set are limited, so reserve your copy today. I've included a secure link for you to make your tax-deductible gift below: [_**https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/dvd-mathematics-and-logic-course**_](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=a15c26d043074d7ba3a99f8ca17d57d0&_e=nScp6_ExYvgConJGLzHsLP1NQBMVx8WGOSrNQ0-tQ3UjLfFo-K4K-B_ufeRHXmfJjmstPnJ9q8zXF4-njMJx637XrYWSua2xMw862VRdqg1UPadU2xkhUpFJeasnzprgaP3kMXCPlSz_mWBcRIuWukYA0uXgY6jueEdYm9YGjQmemyn4YIzyJSLT2Qd79Aj0td53OPONb2Mj3WCQdxKJcrVnUdc7sA9Y3h4OXnU1I-bl865OesHKhHyON3tyt7LaIxgJGK7WLycMUYmEZKe_OyXuvJ1HAOkU2zfjjx4x9MHSweokEBFrjsNfajVXc-D1rJTdk0dI2LJDEnikSI1YXu5GSCq1UNYcWmDmXiRXxbwofsmrdulNbh3YTsRvfby09EisCOmEq8qr0JRgHr731nBjNTdErzU88roknEtAVFA%3D) Thank you for partnering with Hillsdale in its mission on behalf of liberty. Warm regards, Larry P. Arnn President, Hillsdale College Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844

name: IlhanOmar.com
subject: Sorry to be so blunt
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
But we have very little time to waste.
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=413%2E791427%2Euo8TnE)

Friends —
We’re writing to ask if you will make your first donation to Ilhan today to help us stay on track to hit our goals this month. You can use the link below to contribute:
**[https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/AugustDeadline](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-aug23.SAeyb4&refcode=em230806-1-t2&t=2&akid=413%2E791427%2Euo8TnE)**
We’re making progress toward our August fundraising goals, but we still have some work to do to get there. Everyone who chips in gets us one step closer – and might even put us over the top!
We're sorry to be so blunt, but Kevin McCarthy, Donald Trump, and the rest of the GOP establishment have already made it clear that Ilhan is one of their top targets in Congress this cycle. That means there's very little time to waste.
The fact is that building the kind of campaign we need to win takes a lot of resources, and the only way we're doing that is the same way we do everything — together. So please:
**[Can you please chip in $5 or anything you can for Ilhan today? Every dollar will go towards building the kind of campaign we need to send Ilhan back to Congress in 2024.](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/7?c0=io-em-aug23.SAeyb4&refcode=em230806-1-t2&t=3&akid=413%2E791427%2Euo8TnE)**

name: Al Franken
subject: another big announcement
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
| Friend,

A month or two ago I wrote to you with a big announcement: my friend and former Senate counsel Josh Riley is running for Congress in New York’s 19th District – one of the most competitive races in the country.

**Now I’m back with an even bigger announcement: Josh is running for Congress in one of the most competitive races in the country... AND his wife just had a baby!**

[**While Josh is catching sleep in 30-minute increments and cleaning up spit-up, I’m pitching in to make sure his fundraising doesn’t look like a dirty diaper. So I have to ask, will you split a contribution between the Voter Protection Project and Josh’s campaign today?**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/50968?t=6&akid=9693%2E878179%2EojWFxb) If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Josh Riley and VPP will go through immediately: | [CONTRIBUTE $10 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/50969?t=7&akid=9693%2E878179%2EojWFxb)
---
[CONTRIBUTE $25 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/50970?t=8&akid=9693%2E878179%2EojWFxb)
[CONTRIBUTE $50 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/50971?t=9&akid=9693%2E878179%2EojWFxb)
[CONTRIBUTE $100 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/50972?t=10&akid=9693%2E878179%2EojWFxb)
[Or a custom donation »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/50968?t=11&akid=9693%2E878179%2EojWFxb)
You know who’s probably getting a full night's sleep? Josh’s opponent, Marc Molinaro. In fact, he’s probably dreaming about taking away someone’s rights, rolling back gun safety legislation or blocking progress on clean energy – he’s only been in Congress a couple of months and he’s already done all that and more!

We need someone like Josh in Congress – he’s the real deal.
[ ](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/50968?t=12&akid=9693%2E878179%2EojWFxb)
(The really tall guy is Josh.)
After graduating from Harvard Law School, Josh worked on a landmark civil rights lawsuit to help kids from low-income families get access to healthcare. When I was in the Senate, Josh and I passed bills expanding rights for survivors of domestic abuse and providing new funding for addiction treatment and mental health programs. And since then, Josh has fought in the Supreme Court to block the Trump Administration’s Muslim Ban and plans to deport Dreamers.

**Even before he was a dad, Josh was always looking out for the little guy.**

[**So what do you say? Will you split $10 or whatever you can (or more than you can) between the Voter Protection Project and my friend Josh Riley to help him win one of the most important races in the country in 2024?**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/50968?t=13&akid=9693%2E878179%2EojWFxb)

Thanks,

Al

P.S. I like the P.S. because it’s one more opportunity to ask for a donation. [**So donate now!**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/50968?t=14&akid=9693%2E878179%2EojWFxb)
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=9693%2E878179%2EojWFxb). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=9693%2E878179%2EojWFxb).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=9693%2E878179%2EojWFxb) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=15&akid=9693%2E878179%2EojWFxb). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=9693%2E878179%2EojWFxb)
---
[Donate Just One Time »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/124?t=5&akid=9693%2E878179%2EojWFxb)

Peter, Have you seen this email from JVP Action PAC supporting me and Cori Bush as we advocate for Palestinian rights? I’m honored to work for justice alongside my Jewish neighbors. Jewish Voice for Peace Action activists in my community and across the country are standing up to save lives. In Congress, my sister in service Cori Bush has been a crucial ally helping to grow the momentum to end U.S. complicity in Israel’s human rights abuses. As the government of Israel increases violence against Palestinians, pro-apartheid groups are attacking us for speaking out. We cannot let up in our struggle for liberation and to hold the Israeli apartheid government accountable. [**Can you split a donation today between my campaign and Cori Bush’s campaign so we can withstand attacks as we advocate for Palestinians’ human rights?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953zfhtxOic7ZNJAEOuZHds3Sju9vT5DRo2Er9QILo-EVylB8EdnXxXSmVnX2AS_mnW-XukBM2pgSnjlm-8nqNA7eIyHOASK5L_1AR5zAhb7Dg4ZGJokyw5KhodUrIJccz0BC32PedtiOXGKTn8NQuxJLfSFQLSBv1nmC2wbT8OHo86p7L2x3nEn6IzCReNezNKTVaNRwmwzTyDUPNgdbkr-jdc2HBySgwtsgu96NcEm14S4naPKWmXnABNf7gPPE-qB8tmmnZzClPhl-hPty4RsHybMTj6Hnv7fW36x4IxSy_srqCEls18udpnUCoOC34j1MHwX483SU1ftMGnVuJ9uUb1kDzpUXQlnYmpM8E6BhtwtdBq_KJYY4TIOtuOo-KsJOi0VI8lNYUMuiSzLvMtGvbtEUK4S_fpJtGptDSwdlIbbj6bprd_7kd6C3OjIfOaQ/3yi/7mJNbcJfSD6o6m8I5c-wRA/h0/D1c-lMLq_UDHvyRRx4IWaxURQTUaISamDMxCFfOecw8) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately, split evenly between Cori Bush and Rashida Tlaib:_ | | | [Chip in $15 now](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953zfhtxOic7ZNJAEOuZHds3Sju9vT5DRo2Er9QILo-EVyhvx6CoOczO6PXgVmTYu7p9SNm5tEQLeRZ-udmUyAslA4frfCSRWERHszp73BmUYwNisUkD3bwzoMEqYKdHAKIzXI2nujfLSh5DqQnbcqSjiStc3vllrnl4GT4DyRsf1fW8tMWTCf3U6S_XDu0n0pAcg1kBOxwdmGhj--j4viBOeVkWFzlorV79yqM34ZLiAP6N22hiGPthvbX6ukVYkh0gOl_YYTJ0v0zRrekP4xtZ6_IxDnhmbQOdfGsf2-Ew15Z7CrxTypEzrqfy0L3spNtYgAbUzK8Dzan1bL_Ap9UPsmGU2ilhg-50zYyF5L_2tKIHpGrWcejLb71Pw1qmtxk60OuU2LKabSk0c42CN8RYUHg6dKVHCd2wC4Jx3YQpfEvhC9z7rCYxftAl9Y2epG0Wtm9ICgk89fEqQ-M1DlTjnLkDVcggnq-Mu8d-9MRV0_w/3yi/7mJNbcJfSD6o6m8I5c-wRA/h1/Xn_3E9mL80if6Se0nwznZ7eZ9cppBE01C8GknSMcjmw)

Dear Peter,
Cori Bush and Rashida Tlaib are two of the most consistent champions for Palestinian rights on Capitol Hill. **Now, they’re being attacked for their bold stances—and we need to have their backs.**
Time and time again, I have seen Rashida rise on the House floor to defend the basic humanity of her Palestinian community and family. name: Lucas Kunce
subject: re: my new op-ed in Fox News
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Lucas Kunce for Missouri
body:
Tommy Tuberville is jeopardizing our service members, military families, and our national security.
#
Peter,
As a Marine officer doing arms control negotiations, I saw how dangerous a single empty leadership position could be. But right now, Tommy Tuberville is blocking more than 200 military confirmations — and just took five weeks off for Senate recess.
**That’s why I put this op-ed in Fox News — because I want Tommy Tuberville, Josh Hawley, and every other Senate Republican to see it.**
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/35424?refcode=em20230806-3512&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=3512%2E614464%2ERDkjEs)
This email is longer than usual, but I wanted to take a moment this morning to tell you what’s exactly going on and how it impacts service members, military families, and our national security. **Will you please let me explain?**
**[ADD A DONATION NOW »](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/35424?refcode=em20230806-3512&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=3512%2E614464%2ERDkjEs)**
**For the first time in over a century, the United States Marine Corps is without a Commandant — the highest-ranking officer in the Corps.** We're in this situation because Senator Tommy Tuberville has blocked hundreds of military promotions, including the Commandant's.
He claims he’s doing this because government funds have been made available to cover travel for some service members getting abortions. But because I spent 13 years as an active duty Marine and I'm still Lieutenant Colonel in the Reserves, I've been in a lot of conversations about what this means and thought I'd take some time today to lay it out for you.
_As a note, these are my personal observations and may or may not be the same as the Department of Defense._
So first, for a lot of us Marines, this just sucks. Going to the Commandant's house and meeting the Commandant during our initial six-month officer training called The Basic School is one of the highlights of that school.
The same can be said for Marines across the world and at every stage of their training or careers. The Commandant is a big deal for us — someone we look up to, aspire to, and who gives us guidance. So not having a Commandant because some politician in D.C. wants to play games is a morale killer.
But the problem is much bigger than us not having a Commandant. Hundreds of other promotions are backed up with it. **And that doesn't just suck — that has a real impact on our troops and on national security.**
Military life is hard. You move all the time, there's no certainty, there are lots of deployments, you work very long hours, but you do it because you believe in the mission and you believe in the country and taking care of the people who live here.
**And now, because promotions aren't going through, people who've given decades of their lives to this country are being asked or forced to change their plans or not retire.** The one thing they thought they had control over.
People are being frozen in place. They’re not getting the promotion they worked so hard for. Their kids don't know what school they're going to go to next year when the summer ends. Their spouses can't figure out their employment. The list goes on and on, and the impact of that is felt throughout the Corps.
This is wrong. Service members are being used for political leverage, the same way that these politicians recently used us veterans for political leverage by holding up the PACT Act — which takes care of us for burn pit exposure we received in Iraq and Afghanistan. **It's absolutely demoralizing to see them use us like this over and over and over again.**
Senator Tuberville is quite literally punishing military families for things that other people have done that he doesn't like. It's shameful, it's selfish, and I'll be frank, it's stupid because it also damages our national security.
He goes out there and has the nerve to say that promotions and positions don't matter, an acting commander can do just as good a job, and be just as capable with or without the actual nomination going through.
Trust me though, rank and position do matter or they wouldn't exist.
**I learned that myself when I was representing the US in arms control negotiations with NATO allies and Russia from 2016 to 2019.** When you've got American officers with lower ranks going up against generals and admirals in critical meetings and negotiations, that matters.
Our allies, our partners, other countries, and our adversaries notice. Russia and China, they notice.
And now dozens, if not hundreds, of military officers are going to have to explain why the US is sending significantly lower ranks than their counterparts to critical meetings and negotiations around the globe.
When China sends a high-ranking general to meet with a third country and we send a one-star acting commander, that undermines our country, our position, and our national security. It's going to look like we don't care, it's going to look like we aren't serious, and people are going to question our country's resolve.
**Weakening the position of our representatives overseas weakens our national security. Period. And that's exactly what Senator Tuberville is doing.**
We need more folks right now in the media, in Congress, and especially in the Republican Party, to step up and say something — and Senator Tuberville needs to stop putting his personal politics ahead of our troops and ahead of our national security.
These service members, veterans, and their families deserve to have someone looking out for them in the U.S. Senate — and that’s just part of why I’m running to defeat Josh Hawley and flip this seat. But I need your help to do it:
**[So Peter, will you add a donation of whatever you can afford to my grassroots campaign today? I don’t take a single cent from corporate PACs, federal lobbyists, or defense contractors — so you know I’ll always be a voice for real, everyday people in the U.S. Senate.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/35424?refcode=em20230806-3512&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=3512%2E614464%2ERDkjEs)**
If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly:
[Contribute $10](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/35425?refcode=em20230806-3512&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=10&t=4&akid=3512%2E614464%2ERDkjEs) [Contribute $25](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/35425?refcode=em20230806-3512&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=25&t=5&akid=3512%2E614464%2ERDkjEs) [Contribute $30](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/35425?refcode=em20230806-3512&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=30&t=6&akid=3512%2E614464%2ERDkjEs) [Contribute $50](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/35425?refcode=em20230806-3512&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=50&t=7&akid=3512%2E614464%2ERDkjEs) [Contribute $100](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/35425?refcode=em20230806-3512&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=100&t=8&akid=3512%2E614464%2ERDkjEs) [Contribute $250](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/35425?refcode=em20230806-3512&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=250&t=9&akid=3512%2E614464%2ERDkjEs) [Give another amount](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/35424?refcode=em20230806-3512&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=10&akid=3512%2E614464%2ERDkjEs)
---
Thanks for hearing me out,
Lucas Kunce

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lk-email-footer?refcode=em20230806-3512-footer&t=11&refcode2=3512_614464_RDkjEs&akid=3512%2E614464%2ERDkjEs)**

| A native Floridian with blue-collar roots, Ron was raised in Dunedin by his mother, a nurse, and father, who installed cable boxes. He attended Dunedin High School and worked his way through Yale University, where he was the captain of the varsity baseball team. He also graduated with honors from Harvard Law School, where he earned a commission in the U.S. Navy as a JAG officer.

During his active-duty service, Ron deployed to Iraq in support of the SEAL mission in Fallujah, Ramadi and the rest of Al Anbar province. His military decorations include the Bronze Star Medal for Meritorious Service and the Iraq Campaign Medal.
---
Ron also served as a prosecutor in Florida, where he put child predators behind bars. He was elected to Congress in 2012 on a platform of government accountability, advocating for term limits and a balanced budget amendment.

In Congress, DeSantis spearheaded the fight to expose the weaponization of the IRS against conservatives. He also fought to hold accountable the rogue federal agencies involved in manufacturing the "Russia collusion" conspiracy theory. As a veteran, DeSantis was a champion for those who have served our country, pushing for reforms to the VA to better serve veterans and expand support for mental health.

In 2018, Ron DeSantis was elected the 46th Governor of Florida. As governor, he has fought for what's right and taken the arrows for the people he represents. During the darkest days of 2020, when the corrupt establishment, bureaucracy, and corporate media united to force lockdowns and mandates on Americans, DeSantis stood up for freedom - even when it wasn't popular.

Grounded in his unshakeable belief in America's founding principles, Governor DeSantis has enacted a bold agenda to transform Florida into a blueprint for the Nation's revival. Governor DeSantis has delivered win after win on conservative priorities:
* Saving livelihoods from job-killing lockdowns and vaccine mandates,

* Protecting citizens from the harmful impacts of Biden's border crisis by enacting E-Verify and other bold policies against illegal immigration, such as banning sanctuary cities,

* Defending parental rights and eliminating CRT,

* Defunding DEI bureaucracy in higher ed and holding tenured professors accountable,

* Waging war on woke power-grabs like ESG and central bank digital currency,

* Enacting the strongest medical freedom laws in the nation,

* Turning Florida into a model for election integrity,

* Protecting the unborn,

* Standing up for the 2nd Amendment by making Florida a constitutional carry state,

* Expanding education freedom,

* Prohibiting land purchases by the CCP and related individuals and entities,

* Lowering prescription drug costs,

* Holding woke corporations and school unions accountable,

* Creating a universal school choice program,

* Delivering record-breaking tax relief,

* Helping families achieve the American dream of homeownership,

* Supporting law enforcement and our veterans,

* Enacting tougher penalties for drug dealers and sex offenders,

* Appointing seven conservative justices to the Florida Supreme Court,

* ...and so much more.

After leading Florida with common sense and conviction throughout his first term, DeSantis delivered a record-setting victory of nearly 20 points in 2022. He earned his second term by the largest raw vote margin in state history, winning by more than 1.5 million votes.

But history didn't stop there:

Governor DeSantis flipped Miami-Dade County red, winning the majority-Hispanic urban county by double digits. Conventional wisdom assumed this was impossible for a Republican - as recently as 2016, Hillary Clinton won Miami-Dade County by nearly 30 points.

Governor DeSantis flipped several other blue counties red, including Palm Beach County. He won 62 out of 67 counties overall.

Compared to his first statewide election in 2018, Governor DeSantis was up +19% with Independents, up +30% with Hispanics, and up +9% with women.

Governor DeSantis delivered a record-setting victory with battle-tested leadership, a bold policy agenda, and never backing down to corporate media or special interests. As he said at his inauguration: "When the world lost its mind - when common sense suddenly became an uncommon virtue - Florida was a refuge of sanity, a citadel of freedom for our fellow Americans and even for people around the world. In captaining the ship of state, we choose to navigate the boisterous sea of liberty rather than cower in the calm docks of despotism."

Governor DeSantis has ensured that Florida is a free and safe place to raise a family. Thanks to the governor's leadership, Floridians enjoy a low tax burden, quality education opportunities, and a bulwark in place against the Biden Administration's overreach and various elite power grabs that seek to force people to adopt a particular ideology. Thanks to the governor's vision, this year's legislative session delivered the most ambitious conservative policy victories in Florida's history.

This year, Governor DeSantis signed:
* Record tax relief

* ESG prohibitions

* Measures counteracting the influence of the CCP

* The strongest medical freedom protections in the nation.

* Reforms reducing the costs of medications by delivering transparency

* Bold higher education reforms; defunding DEI

* Improvements to our state's infrastructure

* Tough-on-crime, law and order measures, including the death penalty for child rapists

* The strongest measures protecting Florida from Biden's border crisis

* Reforms for paycheck protection , holding accountable wayward school unions

* Prohibitions on centralized digital currencies in Florida

No leader has accomplished more for his state than Governor DeSantis. He is now sharing his bold vision with the country, leading a movement to deliver the Great American Comeback.

Ron is married to Casey DeSantis, an Emmy Award- winning television anchor. Together, they're the proud parents of their daughters, Madison and Mamie, and their son, Mason.
---
|
---

Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, mailing address, occupation, and name of employer for each individual whose aggregate contributions exceed $200 in an election cycle. The maximum that an individual may contribute is $6,600 ($3,300/primary & $3,300/general). The maximum that a couple using a joint account may contribute is $13,200 ($6,600/primary & $6,600/general). A federal multi-candidate PAC may contribute $10,000 ($5,000/primary & $5,000/general). Contribution rules
* I am a U.S. citizen or lawfully admitted permanent resident (i.e., green card holder).
* This contribution is made from my own funds, and funds are not being provided to me by another person or entity for the purpose of making this contribution.
* I am making this contribution with my own personal credit card and not with a corporate or business credit card or a card issued to another person.
* I am at least eighteen years old. I am not, nor am I making this contribution on behalf of, a corporation, labor organization, national bank, foreign national without a green card, a federal contractor, or any other federally impermissible source.

Ron DeSantis was a member of the United States Navy. Use of his military rank, job titles and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

name: Ron DeSantis
subject: Stand with me against woke corporations.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ron DeSantis for President
body:
http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/p/vW53zQeDsN

American taxpayers will not pay the price for ESG & DEI

http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/p/oW53zQeDsN

Friend,

Woke corporations have no business dictating how we live in a
free country. It is fundamentally wrong for corporations, NGOs,
and asset managers to use their power to force a radical agenda
on society. They are pushing racial discrimination in the name of
"diversity," transgenderism in the name of "inclusion," and
Marxism in the name of "equity." Americans never voted for woke
corporate overlords, and we don't have to accept their edicts.

Large corporations push ESG-environmental, social, and corporate
governance-and abuse their unchecked power to push the far-Left
agenda that is destined to fail at the ballot box.

I
am standing in their way.

If
corporate activists want to push political agendas, they should
leave their boardrooms and run for office. Companies need to
focus on doing what they do best: providing goods and services
that people want to buy, not forcing political agendas on
Americans.

As
President, I will not stand for corporations pushing a
destructive left-wing agenda at the expense of American
taxpayers. And I have the track record to prove it.

Last year in Florida, we divested billions of dollars from
BlackRock, a corporation that openly prioritized ESG over
maximizing returns for investors: the taxpayers of my state. When
asset managers are putting politics above fiduciary duty, it is a
leader's job to step up and protect citizens' retirement funds.

And
it worked-just a few days ago, the BlackRock CEO said that as a
consequence of Florida's actions last year, he is now "ashamed"
of promoting ESG. This is a step forward, but we are just getting
started.

As
Governor of the third-largest state, I have stood up to
corporations choosing to put political ideology and utopian
fantasies ahead of the interests of everyday Americans. Now, I am
prepared to take this fight to the national stage as President.

I
have a message for Joe Biden: It is a complete dereliction of
duty to ignore the financial suffering across our country and
expect struggling families to shell out $50,000 on a new electric
vehicle, or even $1,000 on an electric stove, because the climate
lobby and woke mob demands it. That is the true mark of an
out-of-touch puppet.

Join me in my fight against Biden and the woke corporations
trying to make Americans pay the price for the Left's radical
agenda. Please rush in any amount below to help my campaign
achieve its mission and fight back against their attacks.

Donate a $10 gift

Donate a $25 gift

Donate a $47 gift

Donate a $75 gift

Donate a $150 gift

Donate any amount to support the mission

I
will never back down. Decline is a choice, success is attainable,
and freedom is worth fighting for.

In
Freedom,

Ron
DeSantis

Donate any amount to support the mission

Meet Ron DeSantis

A native Floridian with
blue-collar roots, Ron was raised in Dunedin by his mother, a
nurse, and father, who installed cable boxes. He attended Dunedin
High School and worked his way through Yale University, where he
was the captain of the varsity baseball team. He also graduated
with honors from Harvard Law School, where he earned a commission
in the U.S. Navy as a JAG officer.

During his active-duty service,
Ron deployed to Iraq in support of the SEAL mission in Fallujah,
Ramadi and the rest of Al Anbar province. His military
decorations include the Bronze Star Medal for Meritorious Service
and the Iraq Campaign Medal.

Ron
also served as a prosecutor in Florida, where he put child
predators behind bars. He was elected to Congress in 2012 on a
platform of government accountability, advocating for term limits
and a balanced budget amendment.

In
Congress, DeSantis spearheaded the fight to expose the
weaponization of the IRS against conservatives. He also fought to
hold accountable the rogue federal agencies involved in
manufacturing the "Russia collusion" conspiracy theory. As a
veteran, DeSantis was a champion for those who have served our
country, pushing for reforms to the VA to better serve veterans
and expand support for mental health.

In
2018, Ron DeSantis was elected the 46th Governor of Florida. As
governor, he has fought for what's right and taken the arrows for
the people he represents. During the darkest days of 2020, when
the corrupt establishment, bureaucracy, and corporate media
united to force lockdowns and mandates on Americans, DeSantis
stood up for freedom - even when it wasn't popular.

Grounded in his unshakeable belief in America's founding
principles, Governor DeSantis has enacted a bold agenda to
transform Florida into a blueprint for the Nation's revival.
Governor DeSantis has delivered win after win on conservative
priorities:

Saving livelihoods from job-killing lockdowns and vaccine
mandates,

Protecting citizens from the harmful impacts of Biden's border
crisis by enacting E-Verify and other bold policies against
illegal immigration, such as banning sanctuary cities,

Defending parental rights and eliminating CRT,

Defunding DEI bureaucracy in higher ed and holding tenured
professors accountable,

Waging war on woke power-grabs like ESG and central bank digital
currency,

Enacting the strongest medical freedom laws in the nation,

Turning Florida into a model for election integrity,

Protecting the unborn,

Standing up for the 2nd Amendment by making Florida a
constitutional carry state,

Expanding education freedom,

Prohibiting land purchases by the CCP and related individuals and
entities,

Lowering prescription drug costs,

Holding woke corporations and school unions accountable,

Creating a universal school choice program,

Delivering record-breaking tax relief,

Helping families achieve the American dream of homeownership,

Supporting law enforcement and our veterans,

Enacting tougher penalties for drug dealers and sex offenders,

Appointing seven conservative justices to the Florida Supreme
Court,

...and so much more.

After leading Florida with common sense and conviction throughout
his first term, DeSantis delivered a record-setting victory of
nearly 20 points in 2022. He earned his second term by the
largest raw vote margin in state history, winning by more than
1.5 million votes.

But
history didn't stop there:

Governor DeSantis flipped Miami-Dade County red, winning the
majority-Hispanic urban county by double digits. Conventional
wisdom assumed this was impossible for a Republican - as recently
as 2016, Hillary Clinton won Miami-Dade County by nearly 30
points.

Governor DeSantis flipped several other blue counties red,
including Palm Beach County. He won 62 out of 67 counties
overall.

Compared to his first statewide election in 2018, Governor
DeSantis was up +19% with Independents, up +30% with Hispanics,
and up +9% with women.

Governor DeSantis delivered a record-setting victory with
battle-tested leadership, a bold policy agenda, and never backing
down to corporate media or special interests. As he said at his
inauguration: "When the world lost its mind - when common sense
suddenly became an uncommon virtue - Florida was a refuge of
sanity, a citadel of freedom for our fellow Americans and even
for people around the world. In captaining the ship of state, we
choose to navigate the boisterous sea of liberty rather than
cower in the calm docks of despotism."

Governor DeSantis has ensured that Florida is a free and safe
place to raise a family. Thanks to the governor's leadership,
Floridians enjoy a low tax burden, quality education
opportunities, and a bulwark in place against the Biden
Administration's overreach and various elite power grabs that
seek to force people to adopt a particular ideology. Thanks to
the governor's vision, this year's legislative session delivered
the most ambitious conservative policy victories in Florida's
history.

This year, Governor DeSantis signed:

Record tax relief

ESG prohibitions

Measures counteracting the influence of the CCP

The strongest medical freedom protections in the nation.

Reforms reducing the costs of medications by delivering
transparency

Bold higher education reforms; defunding DEI

Improvements to our state's infrastructure

Tough-on-crime, law and order measures, including the death
penalty for child rapists

The strongest measures protecting Florida from Biden's border
crisis

Reforms for paycheck protection , holding accountable wayward
school unions

Prohibitions on centralized digital currencies in Florida

No
leader has accomplished more for his state than Governor
DeSantis. He is now sharing his bold vision with the country,
leading a movement to deliver the Great American Comeback.

Ron
is married to Casey DeSantis, an Emmy Award- winning television
anchor. Together, they're the proud parents of their daughters,
Madison and Mamie, and their son, Mason.

Contributions to Ron DeSantis for President are not deductible as
charitable contributions for federal income tax purposes.
Contributions from corporations, foreign nationals (without
"green cards"), federal government contractors, and national
banks are prohibited. Federal law requires us to use our best
efforts to collect and report the name, mailing address,
occupation, and name of employer for each individual whose
aggregate contributions exceed $200 in an election cycle. The
maximum that an individual may contribute is $6,600
($3,300/primary & $3,300/general). The maximum that a couple
using a joint account may contribute is $13,200 ($6,600/primary &
$6,600/general). A federal multi-candidate PAC may contribute
$10,000 ($5,000/primary & $5,000/general).

Contribution rules

I am a U.S. citizen or lawfully admitted permanent resident
(i.e., green card holder).

This contribution is made from my own funds, and funds are not
being provided to me by another person or entity for the purpose
of making this contribution.

I am making this contribution with my own personal credit card
and not with a corporate or business credit card or a card issued
to another person.

I am at least eighteen years old. I am not, nor am I making this
contribution on behalf of, a corporation, labor organization,
national bank, foreign national without a green card, a federal
contractor, or any other federally impermissible source.

Ron
DeSantis was a member of the United States Navy. Use of his
military rank, job titles and photographs in uniform does not
imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department
of Defense.

Paid for by Ron DeSantis for President

PO Box 3696

Tallahassee, FL 32315

Governor DeSantis has delivered win after win on conservative priorities: Saving livelihoods from job-killing lockdowns and vaccine mandates, Protecting citizens from the harmful impacts of Biden's border crisis by enacting E-Verify and other bold policies against illegal immigration, such as banning sanctuary cities, Defending parental rights and eliminating CRT, Defunding DEI bureaucracy in higher ed and holding tenured professors accountable, Waging war on woke power-grabs like ESG and central bank digital currency, Enacting the strongest medical freedom laws in the nation, Turning Florida into a model for election integrity, Protecting the unborn, Standing up for the 2nd Amendment by making Florida a constitutional carry state, Expanding education freedom, Prohibiting land purchases by the CCP and related individuals and entities, Lowering prescription drug costs, Holding woke corporations and school unions accountable, Creating a universal school choice program, Delivering record-breaking tax relief, Helping families achieve the American dream of homeownership, Supporting law enforcement and our veterans, Enacting tougher penalties for drug dealers and sex offenders, Appointing seven conservative justices to the Florida Supreme Court, ...and so much more. After leading Florida with common sense and conviction throughout his first term, DeSantis delivered a record-setting victory of nearly 20 points in 2022. He earned his second term by the largest raw vote margin in state history, winning by more than 1.5 million votes. But history didn't stop there: Governor DeSantis flipped Miami-Dade County red, winning the majority-Hispanic urban county by double digits. Conventional wisdom assumed this was impossible for a Republican - as recently as 2016, Hillary Clinton won Miami-Dade County by nearly 30 points. Governor DeSantis flipped several other blue counties red, including Palm Beach County. He won 62 out of 67 counties overall. Compared to his first statewide election in 2018, Governor DeSantis was up +19% with Independents, up +30% with Hispanics, and up +9% with women. Governor DeSantis delivered a record-setting victory with battle-tested leadership, a bold policy agenda, and never backing down to corporate media or special interests. As he said at his inauguration: "When the world lost its mind - when common sense suddenly became an uncommon virtue - Florida was a refuge of sanity, a citadel of freedom for our fellow Americans and even for people around the world. In captaining the ship of state, we choose to navigate the boisterous sea of liberty rather than cower in the calm docks of despotism." Governor DeSantis has ensured that Florida is a free and safe place to raise a family. Thanks to the governor's leadership, Floridians enjoy a low tax burden, quality education opportunities, and a bulwark in place against the Biden Administration's overreach and various elite power grabs that seek to force people to adopt a particular ideology. Thanks to the governor's vision, this year's legislative session delivered the most ambitious conservative policy victories in Florida's history. This year, Governor DeSantis signed: Record tax relief ESG prohibitions Measures counteracting the influence of the CCP The strongest medical freedom protections in the nation. Reforms reducing the costs of medications by delivering transparency Bold higher education reforms; defunding DEI Improvements to our state's infrastructure Tough-on-crime, law and order measures, including the death penalty for child rapists The strongest measures protecting Florida from Biden's border crisis Reforms for paycheck protection , holding accountable wayward school unions Prohibitions on centralized digital currencies in Florida No leader has accomplished more for his state than Governor DeSantis. He is now sharing his bold vision with the country, leading a movement to deliver the Great American Comeback. Ron is married to Casey DeSantis, an Emmy Award- winning television anchor. Together, they're the proud parents of their daughters, Madison and Mamie, and their son, Mason. Contributions from corporations, foreign nationals (without "green cards"), federal government contractors, and national banks are prohibited. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, mailing address, occupation, and name of employer for each individual whose aggregate contributions exceed $200 in an election cycle. The maximum that an individual may contribute is $6,600 ($3,300/primary & $3,300/general). The maximum that a couple using a joint account may contribute is $13,200 ($6,600/primary & $6,600/general). A federal multi-candidate PAC may contribute $10,000 ($5,000/primary & $5,000/general). Contribution rules I am a U.S. citizen or lawfully admitted permanent resident (i.e., green card holder). name: Kamala Harris
subject: 58 years ago today
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Biden for President
body:
Today marks 58 years since President Lyndon B. Johnson signed the Voting Rights Act into law -- one of the most important pieces of legislation in our nation's history. | | |
---
Peter, Today marks 58 years since President Lyndon B. Johnson signed the _Voting Rights Act_ into law -- one of the most important pieces of legislation in our nation's history. The most fundamental right of our citizenship is the right to vote, and the _Voting Rights Act_ guaranteed that right for all Americans, prohibiting racial discrimination in voting. The impact was immediate: It’s estimated that in the five years after the law was passed, almost as many Black Americans registered to vote in some southern states as in the _100 years_ before the law was passed. Today, we reflect on that impact, and on the work of the generations before us who made this landmark legislation possible. But today we also recommit to the fights ahead, **because our fight to protect, strengthen, and expand the freedom to vote continues.** In the last few years, we’ve witnessed a flurry of anti-voter legislation in states across the country, even making it illegal to give people food and water who are in line to vote. Make no mistake, these laws are designed to make it harder for people to cast their votes and to have their say in our democracy. **Make no mistake, Peter: We will keep fighting back, and we’ll keep working to make sure everyone who wants to make their voice heard has the opportunity to do so.** [That’s why I’m hoping you’ll join our team for our Summer Training Academy, which starts on August 12. This is an important opportunity to learn how to make an impact locally in your community and across the country, as we look ahead to 2024:](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/XlxiKm-amnZREMhISCI1HXgISUQ6kaMw7miBXrv1T3OTI34gvs80HB63Y4ZPo4et1YyP56z4zeeU9awJtpc4DQW2VD9Iuxxj2OMJyjNJfnAozfVlixFCr4L3LgTSbfp-8Ju_9aDtY1JWhgDiscABAGnZtfDrL-OYzDhnk_yl8k3rtKj8MklbSXbL-K5HMhMZmERGRHYHDysi-EqvAnOQzGq3q27eO_Tl26ri_hYtCw5htSxaI8VIqEHg9-8120F7hntmOIocIHkU9A3XBhQ7QhnCY3AUoc8IvvU8vQOIe5fhinaIs0FenrFnGW8mYSeRXoALsh0WYZtD4442JWnS_Q/3yi/l9ei7i0kRGijlB8G145E0Q/h0/slLQiQ6aWJrsI_vU5-1TGlHoO2d31S5Iv2-ifVPIqPI)
| | | [SIGN UP](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/XlxiKm-amnZREMhISCI1HXgISUQ6kaMw7miBXrv1T3OTI34gvs80HB63Y4ZPo4et1YyP56z4zeeU9awJtpc4DQW2VD9Iuxxj2OMJyjNJfnAozfVlixFCr4L3LgTSbfp-8Ju_9aDtY1JWhgDiscABAGnZtfDrL-OYzDhnk_yl8k3qe7jMYSI_ZMmmfpR0r_yU3_fLDW6aKXs1rnQqDi8VnhKrunjRZC7JfvJohEFUZC0ZH_NkARQhqP0mYRNoEsgHZcuQ_AfZqteEwplFA4QxupFkyEGllZ39v7xhK5l4nB9FETyRpuqfzu3dKtpxQMyChLDnXvffkuCYv1E2nNGfqg/3yi/l9ei7i0kRGijlB8G145E0Q/h1/4QeO43T9WoRyO-E5P5j63E3m4GZQMKNEY3CLC3JMzBA)
---

Generations of Americans have fought to strengthen and expand freedoms in our country -- they’ve done it through organizing, through connecting with people in their communities and beyond, and working together to make a difference.
Now, it’s up to us to continue that work. The fight for our rights must be fought and won with each generation.
Thanks,
Kamala
Kamala Harris
Vice President of the United States

clean_body: Today marks 58 years since President Lyndon B. Johnson signed the Voting Rights Act into law -- one of the most important pieces of legislation in our nation's history. | | | Peter, Today marks 58 years since President Lyndon B. Johnson signed the Voting Rights Act into law -- one of the most important pieces of legislation in our nation's history. The most fundamental right of our citizenship is the right to vote, and the Voting Rights Act guaranteed that right for all Americans, prohibiting racial discrimination in voting. The impact was immediate: It’s estimated that in the five years after the law was passed, almost as many Black Americans registered to vote in some southern states as in the 100 years before the law was passed. Today, we reflect on that impact, and on the work of the generations before us who made this landmark legislation possible. But today we also recommit to the fights ahead, because our fight to protect, strengthen, and expand the freedom to vote continues. In the last few years, we’ve witnessed a flurry of anti-voter legislation in states across the country, even making it illegal to give people food and water who are in line to vote. Make no mistake, these laws are designed to make it harder for people to cast their votes and to have their say in our democracy. Make no mistake, Peter: We will keep fighting back, and we’ll keep working to make sure everyone who wants to make their voice heard has the opportunity to do so. | | | Generations of Americans have fought to strengthen and expand freedoms in our country -- they’ve done it through organizing, through connecting with people in their communities and beyond, and working together to make a difference. Now, it’s up to us to continue that work. The fight for our rights must be fought and won with each generation. Thanks, Kamala Kamala Harris Vice President of the United States

#

Friends -
We are writing to humbly ask if you can make a $1 donation to GIFFORDS PAC today. Yes, just $1.
$1 to help us have a strong start to August as we work to beat our summer fundraising slump.
$1 to help us continue fighting for gun safety legislation at the federal level and in every state nationwide—legislation that has the power to save lives.
$1 to send a strong message to the gun lobby that we aren’t backing down in the fight to keep our communities safe from gun violence.
We know that $1 may not seem like the kind of money that can do all those things. But when everyone contributes their $1, it all adds up to make a big difference.
So please:

name: Katie Porter
subject: As a former teacher
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Senate
body:
Here’s how I view the job
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/14558?t=1&akid=27745%2E3422442%2EnsqHkl)
_Being an elected official is a “teaching and learning” job._
**My job is to learn from Californians about problems they’re facing**. This week I spent time in the Central Valley, getting to really listen and learn from people there. I visited farms and orchards in Arvin and Shafter, hearing how Congress can support our state’s agriculture industry and the workers who literally keep food on our tables.
**It’s also my job to teach people about what’s going on in Congress and how it relates to them**. I know most people only tune in if they have a few extra minutes—and that’s why I try to get my message across quickly and effectively, whether on TV or in a Congressional Hearing that you might catch a clip of on Youtube. It’s our job as elected officials to engage voters, which means showing up in all areas, including places that are often overlooked.
I love campaigning because it involves so much teaching and learning. Your support allows me to stay on the road, connecting with the voters we’ll need to win this competitive race.
[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230806?refcode=EMAK07_LOB_20230807_V1_F&refcode2=27745_3422442_nsqHkl&akid=27745%2E3422442%2EnsqHkl)**
Thanks for being a part of Team Porter,
Katie

Katie is running for the U.S. Senate because Californians deserve a warrior fighting for them in Washington. Our campaign is people-powered, we don’t take a dime from corporate PACs, federal lobbyists, or executives from Big Oil or Big Pharma. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-footer?refcode=EMAK07_LOB_20230807_V1_F&t=2&refcode2=27745_3422442_nsqHkl&akid=27745%2E3422442%2EnsqHkl)**

Friend, Today marks one year until Michigan’s 2024 August primary, and we’re gearing up to launch our campaign for Rashida Tlaib’s re-election to Congress. **Last year, pro-apartheid groups like AIPAC and affiliated PACs spent over $1 million attacking Rashida, flooding her district with misinformation and attack ads to mislead voters.** We know she’ll be a target again because she keeps standing firm in her values, including putting up the Palestinian flag outside her office in Congress and telling the truth about Israel’s apartheid government. She keeps putting people first over dark money PACs, corporate polluters, and others influencing politics to maintain the unjust status quo. **We need to be ready to counter misinformation by bringing Rashida’s message to as many constituents as possible. So we’re building out our field campaign for 2024.** Rashida comes from grassroots activism, and as a public servant deeply rooted in her community, she prioritizes speaking with her constituents directly—from hosting events in her district’s communities to knocking on doors. **To start expanding our campaign’s field team, we’ve got to start raising money now. Since Rashida doesn’t take any money from corporate PACs, our campaign relies on grassroots donations from people like you.** [**Can you chip in now for Rashida’s 2024 re-election campaign so she can stay in Congress fighting for all of us?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953z5cM0Umj1Y9p8A22ppKIcp2uEl8PbKd_5niZpdCwVQWZb5jaTQCFGgK7hjrERx9qs_8gXXuV0jcLVTJBKZ8iOrBMyydpO0Kh1nG6hCbM5sh2SJwDdsYB2MDTnZ2OCP6tJ5a4wyyiRY_WVYVnaLge9Ee-pGnTrYMly1Q3e6PLQQsXggu2c9wO7uwhaxdlqWce6yVwIbQjTibvPfJfC7kP5VjYkOnSoYPfJQTl1zlXjIyig6lPU3xvr9cIGU6XH0I4JySjBCSrh0HFmzGih3FwissY_YEDlshDaMsjjxHn_WtY8_gOsDDREKEbwQsNlLY9A/3yi/yq-1qNoIR7ajaEm4uqTgxg/h1/DeX2nVHn8T4s2y_Xn2fl4pDN-j8h3lnJALqNxYQU6XY) | [CHIP IN NOW](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953z5cM0Umj1Y9p8A22ppKIcp2uEl8PbKd_5niZpdCwVQWcH9yBn-C28rI1d3zuLkEZ6oNt_8rrVSBWagT1PLHFfzTl2U8TOjXCjfwORUzuOA4fl08is6gPFf9_Du2dboO1x8_Pd-mNPyqDczD4JHKs4ZcFx-wiZUWO1b0GQ4hqu_xIVyp9i5hGQvZ-TPGaTqfZotBv3nO0ZVHf3za0HLuax_AisWdoYdkNMfPv9YuQtqAaIVA0hZc0JJ-Ycwry9feg-G8qWuxENw0-IhKr-LE4qaTi1MWAPeXar-LSpfWTwzTMX3g4J1a5Dn9k22tWFOu4k/3yi/yq-1qNoIR7ajaEm4uqTgxg/h2/fA3ytmsNHHJs5dC2rA2XGqFmQCdPZEx8xdTGNqTfCBo)

When we turn out people to vote in Rashida’s district, we’re turning out some of the most progressive voters in Michigan.
**As a swing state, Michigan’s been targeted by right-wing voter suppression—especially the Black and brown voters in and around Detroit.**
But with our voter education and election protection efforts, we’ve helped elect not only Rashida but other progressives—and we’ve helped secure important statewide victories such as protecting abortion rights in the state Constitution. Now, Democrats lead the state government in Michigan, which has led to important policy wins.
**Getting people out to vote in Michigan is especially important next year, since 2024 is a presidential election year and Michigan has an open Senate seat.**
Rashida led the fight to get Trump impeached twice, and we helped vote him out of office. He just got indicted again, but it doesn’t seem to matter to his supporters. Now, we are up against increasingly fascist Republican presidential candidates hell-bent on taking away our freedoms, dividing us, and ruling over us rather than representing us.
For example, Florida’s Governor has attacked trans and queer people and is now defending Florida’s Board of Education, which just approved new Black history standards so that public school students will be taught that enslaved Black people benefited from slavery. **This is disgusting white supremacy.**
Attempts to ban books and whitewash our history are happening across the country. And Republicans are also planning to ban abortion nationwide.
**Rashida’s bold voice and outspoken leadership are more critical now than ever, and it’s also more important than ever to turn out the vote in Michigan.**
[**Please donate $5 or more today to help us re-elect Rashida and get out the vote in Michigan to protect our rights.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953z5cM0Umj1Y9p8A22ppKIcp2uEl8PbKd_5niZpdCwVQWc4SOJyfMe2CTmAs7N9K0n1_NSjzGqzhU1TG27dSdoVLZNF9T7zQ6RsabSOHNaT-p-55dCNyRWLzK4NYJBP0avYI0avCjyn3OGWW8sQrWF4Rv39m_XUzHlDG8dfPziaIiieQuRdoNHtaTyxly5Yth6c-E04ultbQ9MBTqnwbeedjSBPb47XqDi5DQ5jRkCjYC0H6VQj8LmUTgpbTRLHKjuorQLGfN-oocsXmHA-u3-UDfLRfe_SWCaFLLd0ZDHRhdyP9lKlTlCxZ6e3z1mB63LA/3yi/yq-1qNoIR7ajaEm4uqTgxg/h3/bS7tSDtbQAtObjYP2vqDqi52A1_41RN_fwUoHXdplBo)

subject: Top News: King County debacles
body:
The August Primary is now behind us, and the races are on for the General Election. And, as always, we have you covered with what's going on in your own backyard. Read on... | | [](http://click.shiftwa.org/ct/62108981:sydbseDNc:m:1:2610672541:1E0B65258B77139BD657BE8409F6C4FC:r "Shift Washington")
---
|
Friends, The August Primary is now behind us, and the races are on for the General Election. And, as always, we have you covered with what's going on in your own backyard. Read on... ** ** **This week 's Newsmaker Interview...** This week's interview is with Jackson Maynard, Executive Director of the Citizen Action Defense Fund (CADF), a non-profit organization in Washington State that aims to ensure government compliance with the rules and protection of constitutional rights. CADF asked Washington State Auditor Pat McCarthy to investigate financial and administrative issues within the King County Prosecuting Attorney's juvenile diversion program, but received a lackluster response from the Auditor. Maynard discussed his concerns about the program's problems and other legal actions CADF is taking to hold Governor Jay Inslee and Democrat lawmakers accountable for their extreme policies. [Read more](http://click.shiftwa.org/ct/62108982:sydbseDNc:m:1:2610672541:1E0B65258B77139BD657BE8409F6C4FC:r). **This week 's top story...** Marc Dones, the first CEO of the King County Regional Homelessness Authority (KCRHA), resigned after two years. During his tenure, homelessness in King County increased, and complaints arose about KCRHA's unresponsiveness in cleaning up homeless encampments near schools. Despite receiving tens of millions in public funds, the authority failed to effectively address homelessness, with more people dying on the streets than moving into permanent housing. As support eroded, Dones resigned from his $247,000-per-year position. The search for his replacement is underway. The KCRHA approved a search committee, likely to be composed of members from the same liberal establishment that previously failed to find a suitable leader. [Read more](http://click.shiftwa.org/ct/62108983:sydbseDNc:m:1:2610672541:1E0B65258B77139BD657BE8409F6C4FC:r). **In case you missed it ...**
Spokane voters successfully qualified an anti-camping initiative for the November ballot. However, the initiative's fate hangs in the balance due to a lawsuit filed by Knoll Lowney, a Seattle-area lawyer known for frivolous attacks on conservatives. Lowney argues that citizens cannot use local ballot measures to override the authority granted to municipal governments by the Washington Legislature. If the lawsuit is successful, it could nullify the efforts of Spokane voters and prevent the initiative from going to the ballot. [Read this story and more](http://click.shiftwa.org/ct/62108984:sydbseDNc:m:1:2610672541:1E0B65258B77139BD657BE8409F6C4FC:r). **This week 's bit of inspiration...** Take a moment to enjoy the beautiful photography of a sunset over Bandon Beach in Oregon, courtesy of Former U.S. Rep. Rod Chandler. | [ DONATE ](http://click.shiftwa.org/ct/62108985:sydbseDNc:m:1:2610672541:1E0B65258B77139BD657BE8409F6C4FC:r)
---
|
This message was intended for: [dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com) and sent from Shift Washington. If you would like to send this message to some of your friends, you can [click here](http://click.shiftwa.org/forwardthis/ft.php?mID=14264096&em=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&ch=49000b887de404a22f48ed48dd2d4356&schema=echo3) to do so.

You can also stay up to date on our efforts to shift the debate in Washington State by following us on [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/shiftwa/) & [Twitter](https://twitter.com/ShiftWA). If you believe you received this message in error or no longer wish to receive emails from Shift Washington, [click to unsubscribe](http://click.shiftwa.org/p/osydbseDNc).
---
